title: Chicken Satay

overview.description: Marinated chicken skewers grilled until lightly charred and served with a rich Indonesian-style peanut satay sauce.

ingredients: 700g boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into strips
1 tsp palm sugar (or light brown sugar)
method: Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.
Blitz the roasted peanuts into a coarse powder.
Blend the garlic, shallot, chilli (if using), tamarind, kecap manis and soy sauce into a paste.
Cook the paste in a saucepan for 2–3 minutes.
Add the coconut milk and ground peanuts.
Simmer gently for 10 minutes until thick and creamy.
Grill, barbecue or cook on a griddle over a high heat, turning regularly, until lightly charred and cooked through.
Serve immediately with the warm satay sauce.

planner.freezer-label: chicken freezes well, sauce freezes well separately

learning.did-you-know: Satay originated in Indonesia and spread throughout Southeast Asia, with each country developing its own variation.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to marinate chicken, make an authentic peanut sauce from roasted peanuts and grill meat over a high heat.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A fantastic introduction to Indonesian cooking using ingredients that also appear throughout the Southeast Asian recipes.
chapman-tips: Chicken thighs stay much juicier than breast over high heat.
Roasted peanuts produce a richer, fresher sauce than peanut butter.
Kecap manis gives the satay its characteristic sweet, caramelised flavour.
Cook over very high heat to develop lightly charred edges.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the marinated chicken or the finished sauce separately.
notes.leftovers: Excellent served cold in wraps or sliced over noodle salads.
